data.title: Crypto mining firm SOLAI's Q3 revenue falls, net loss narrows
data.description:
Overview

  • SOLAI Q3 revenue declines 8.3% yr/yr due to increased mining difficulty and machine maintenance

  • Net loss for Q3 reduced significantly yr/yr, aided by positive cryptocurrency asset value changes

  • Operating costs increased 23.3% yr/yr, driven by higher electricity and depreciation expenses

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific financial guidance for future quarters or the full year

Result Drivers

  • SOLANA TREASURY STRATEGY - Solana treasury strategy actively generating yield, with positive community engagement on validator node

  • DOLAI STABLECOIN VALIDATION - Successful validation of core technology for DOLAI stablecoin, paving way for cross-border payment use cases

  • DATA CENTER REVENUE GROWTH - Increase in data center revenue due to new customers, offsetting declines in self-mining revenue

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q3 Revenue

$4.40 mln

Q3 Adjusted Net Income

-$2.40 mln

Q3 Net Income

-$2.50 mln

Q3 Adjusted Operating Income

-$2.70 mln

Q3 Operating Income

-$2.70 mln

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "hold"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the blockchain & cryptocurrency peer group is "buy."

Press Release:

data.title: U.S. Crude Production Defies Low Prices, Rig Count — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. oil production continues to hit records despite low prices and a rig count that's down by a third from a recent peak in December of 2022. "There's still a misplaced sense that oil production growth is stalling out and reversing towards negative levels. Unfortunately it's a product of where the rig count is and the petroleum supply monthlies lagging reality," says Vikas Dwivedi, Global Energy Strategist at Macquarie Group." While some people think production is 13.4 million or 13.5 million barrels a day, "we think it's around 14 million barrels a day." Baker Hughes reported active oil rigs this week at 419, two more than last week but 60 fewer than a year ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Flotek Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >FTK
data.description:
Flotek Industries Inc. (FTK) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on November 21, 2025.

On November 19, 2025, the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors (the "Board") of Flotek Industries, Inc. (the "Company") approved grants of equity awards to each of Dr. Ryan Ezell and J. Bond Clement, the Company's Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer, respectively. Such equity award grants are comprised of restricted stock units ("RSUs") and performance-based restricted stock units ("PRSUs"), with Dr. Ezell being granted 39,532 RSUs and 59,298 PRSUs and Mr. Clement being granted 17,969 RSUs and 26,953 PRSUs.

The RSUs will vest in three equal annual installments starting on the first anniversary of the date of grant. Up to half of the PRSUs will vest if, and to the extent, the Company's earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization, and certain other adjustments meets or exceeds certain thresholds during the performance period of January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2026, subject to continued employment through December 31, 2027. Up to half of the PRSUs will vest, if, and to the extent, the Company's total shareholder return relative to the Russell 2000 Index – Oil Equipment and Services, measured over a performance period from January 1, 2026 through December 31, 2027, meets or exceeds certain thresholds.

The RSU grants were made pursuant to the Company's Form of Restricted Stock Unit Award Grant Notice and Restricted Stock Unit Award Agreement, which is filed as Exhibit 10.1 hereto. The PRSU grants were made pursuant to the Company's Form of Restricted Stock Unit Award Grant Notice and Restricted Stock Unit Award Agreement, which is filed as Exhibit 10.2 hereto. The grants of RSUs and PRSUs were collectively made pursuant to the Company's 2018 Long-Term Incentive Plan, as amended (the "2018 LTIP"), which is filed as Exhibit 10.3 hereto. The above description of the equity award grants is a summary only and is qualified in its entirety by reference to the relevant documents attached hereto and incorporated by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/928054/000092805425000113/ftk-20251119.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/928054/000092805425000113/0000928054-25-000113-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: SNDL Receives Approval to Renew Share Repurchase Program
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Cannabis company SNDL received approval to renew its share repurchase program from the Canadian Securities Exchange.

Under the plan, the company is authorized to buyback up to 100 million Canadian dollars ($70.9 million) of its outstanding common shares. The company is limited to purchasing a maximum of about 24.5 million shares under the program, representing 10% of its public float at the time the program commences.

The share repurchase program will expire on November 20, 2026.

The company's shares closed the day's regular trading session on the Nasdaq at $1.61, gaining about 1.2%.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Moody's grants Italy its first rating upgrade in 23 years
data.description:
Global ratings agency Moody's on Friday upgraded Italy's sovereign rating to "Baa2" from "Baa3", citing the country's consistent track-record of political and policy stability.

This marks the sovereign's first upgrade by Moody's in twenty three years.

data.title: Pfizer Sells $6 Billion of Notes
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Pfizer sold an aggregate of $6 billion in notes due in the coming years.

The pharmaceutical giant sold $500 million of floating rate notes and $1 billion in 3.9% notes, both due in 2027, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.

Pfizer also sold $1 billion of 4.2% notes due of 2030, $1.25 billion of 4.5% notes due of 2032 and $1.25 billion of 4.9% notes due in 2035. It also sold $500 million each of both 5.6% notes due in 2055 and 5.7% notes due in 2065.

Citigroup, Deutsche Bank, JPMorgan and Mizuho Securities are listed as the offering's underwriters.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com
